name: Green Chili Sauce
recipeIngredient: 6 to 8 Anaheim or poblano chili peppers (seeded and chopped)
or 3 4.5 oz cans of chili peppers (chopped)
recipeInstructions:
If using fresh chili peppers, roast over an open flame or under a broiler until black and blistered. Place in a paper bag and let steam for 5 minutes. Remove from the bag and run under water to remove the charred skin. Remove the tops; slit open and remove membranes and seeds. You should have about 1–2 cups of chili peppers.
Place the chili peppers in a blender container or food processor bowl with the garlic, onion, flour, and garlic salt. Cover and blend on low speed until smooth. If the mixture does not blend easily, add about half a cup of the water to help it blend.
Pour the blended sauce into a medium saucepan. Add the remaining 1½ cups of water. Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, for about 30 minutes or until slightly thickened and bubbly.
Reduce the heat and simmer gently, uncovered, until reduced to 2½ cups.
Use the sauce on Breakfast Burritos.
